originalTitle: Madagascar military leader dissolves government in surprise move
title: Madagascar military leader dissolves government in surprise move
content: Col Randrianirina, who seized power last year, did not give a reason for dissolving his government.

originalTitle: Ghana calls on Commonwealth to condemn attack on its peacekeepers in Lebanon
title: Ghana's Brave Helpers Are Safe!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you important news from our amazing continent, Africa. Today, we have a story about some very brave people from Ghana, our brothers and sisters in West Africa.

Our Brave Soldiers

Some of our brave soldiers from Ghana went to a faraway country called Lebanon. They are called 'peacekeepers'. What do peacekeepers do? They are like superheroes without capes! Their job is to help keep peace in places where people are having a difficult time. They help everyone stay safe and make friends again. Imagine you and your friend are not getting along. A peacekeeper is like a kind teacher who helps you both talk and become friends again. That's what our soldiers do for grown-ups in different countries! They wear special uniforms and drive special cars. They help people build homes, they share food, and they make sure children can go to school safely. They work very hard to make the world a better place. Did you know? Ghana has sent peacekeepers to many countries around the world for a very long time! We are known for having brave and kind soldiers who help others.

Where is Ghana?

Ghana is a beautiful country right here in Africa! It's in West Africa, near countries like Togo and Ivory Coast. Ghana is known for its friendly people, yummy cocoa (which makes chocolate!), and beautiful beaches. Ghana is a very peaceful country itself, and its people love to help others. That's why our soldiers volunteer to go and help people in other parts of the world who need it. Did you know? Ghana was the first country in sub-Saharan Africa to become free from colonial rule in 1957! It's a country with a lot of history and pride.

Where is Lebanon?

Lebanon is a country very far away from Ghana. It's in a place called the Middle East. It's near countries like Syria and Israel. Lebanon also has beautiful mountains and a lovely sea. Sometimes, countries have problems, like big arguments, and people get sad or hurt. That's when peacekeepers like our Ghanaian soldiers step in to help calm things down and make sure everyone is safe. They work with the people there to build a better future. Did you know? Lebanon is a very old country, even older than many countries we know today! People have lived there for thousands and thousands of years.

What Happened to Our Brave Soldiers?

Something sad happened while our peacekeepers were doing their important job in Lebanon. Four of our brave soldiers were hurt. This is called an 'attack'. It means someone tried to hurt them while they were working to help people. But here is the good news: Ghana's army says that all four soldiers are now 'stable'. This means they are safe, they are getting better, and doctors are taking very good care of them. Phew! We are so happy to hear they are okay and recovering. It's very brave of them to keep helping even when things are dangerous. They are true heroes for putting their lives at risk to help others. Did you know? Soldiers train very hard to be strong and smart. They learn how to help people in many different ways, not just by being tough.

What is the Commonwealth?

Now, Ghana has asked something special from a big group called the 'Commonwealth'. What is the Commonwealth? It's like a very big family of countries that used to be connected to the United Kingdom a long, long time ago. Many countries in Africa, like Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, and South Africa, are part of this family. They all talk to each other, help each other, and share ideas. It's like having many friends all over the world! Did you know? The Commonwealth has 56 member countries from all over the world! That's a lot of friends!

Why Did Ghana Call the Commonwealth?

Ghana asked the Commonwealth to 'condemn' the attack. 'Condemn' is a big word, but it just means to say very, very strongly that something is wrong. Ghana wants all its friends in the Commonwealth family to agree that it is very bad to hurt peacekeepers. When a country hurts peacekeepers, it makes it harder for them to do their job of helping people. Ghana wants everyone to know that peacekeepers should be safe while they are helping to make peace. They are not there to fight, but to help. By asking the Commonwealth to condemn the attack, Ghana is showing that it cares deeply about its soldiers and about peace around the world. It's like saying, "Please, let's all agree that this is wrong, so it doesn't happen again."

Why is This Important?

This news is important because it reminds us how brave our Ghanaian soldiers are. They go far away from home to help people they don't even know. They put their own safety aside to bring peace to others. We must always remember to support our peacekeepers and respect the work they do. When they are safe, they can do an even better job of helping children and families in difficult places. Let's all hope for peace for our brave soldiers and for everyone around the world. Our Ghanaian peacekeepers are true stars! They are getting better, and that's the best news of all!
summary: Four brave Ghanaian soldiers were hurt while helping to keep peace in a faraway country called Lebanon, but they are now safe and recovering, and Ghana wants its friends in the Commonwealth to say that hurting peacekeepers is very wrong.
whyMatters: This is important because our brave soldiers help make the world a safer and happier place for everyone, and we must make sure they are kept safe while they do their very important job.
